problem_statement: Nested Mind exposes a distributed lease adapter registry that classifies lease backends by adapter mode, fencing-token support, compare-and-swap support, production readiness, required environment, and delegated gateway status before a distributed lease claim report can be trusted. Mullu Control Plane now has distributed lease claim receipts, but it lacks the adapter registry receipt boundary that binds backend capability and claim receipt hash before a scheduler lease adapter can be treated as ready, delegated, or blocked.
success_criteria: Distributed lease adapter registry receipts bind registry hash, capability hash, backend kind, adapter mode, production readiness, fencing-token support, compare-and-swap support, required environment, and claim receipt hash
SQLite compare-and-swap capability can be classified as adapter_ready without performing a live SQLite mutation
External HTTP gateway capability can be classified as adapter_delegated without performing a live HTTP request
Native Postgres, Redis, etcd, and Consul capabilities block admission when production readiness is false
Backends that require fencing tokens block when the selected adapter capability does not support fencing tokens
Underlying distributed lease claim receipt violations block adapter capability admission before any backend readiness claim
Raw token, JWT, or private-key shaped material in registry or capability metadata blocks receipt creation
The distributed lease adapter registry receipt is schema-backed, indexed in the governance protocol manifest, and included in proof coverage matrix witnesses
No live SQLite, Postgres, Redis, etcd, Consul, HTTP gateway, scheduler mutation, worker dispatch, deployment, or production readiness claim is introduced
non_goals: no live distributed lease backend call
no production readiness claim beyond local receipt contract classification
constraints: adapter registry evaluation must remain deterministic and non-live
adapter registry receipts must bind the underlying distributed lease claim receipt hash
native client adapters must not be treated as ready unless production_ready is true
external gateway adapters must be represented as delegation boundaries, not local execution
fencing-token policy must fail closed when adapter capability does not support fencing tokens
raw token, JWT, or private-key shaped material must fail closed
evidence_required: focused distributed lease adapter registry test receipt
standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
